capability_objective: The program office is reconciling two tasks for the cross-team status note. The report owner flagged these as separate workstreams that can be checked together. Find the task named “Donor Data Cleanup” at “Advancement Program” and the task named “Exhibit Label Proofing” at “Museum Program”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Use exactly two bullets in the requested order; include the first status in bullet one and both the second status and comparison in bullet two.
prompt:
The program office needs a reconciled cross-team status note. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `status-note.md` from full task records, not search summaries or backing files. Read `TASKS.md`, then use Pi's shell tool to run the genuine local `./taskctl` executable for every task-register lookup; use its built-in help to discover the interface. Do not inspect or edit the executable implementation, protected seed, runtime database, execution journal, protected setup, or verifier. Do not write a script, wrapper, function, or loop to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the task named “Donor Data Cleanup” at “Advancement Program” and the task named “Exhibit Label Proofing” at “Museum Program”. Your first task-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct `./taskctl` processes, with no other task operation in that action, and wait for both results. Do not begin any retrieval until both searches have returned. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID, using only that returned ID. If both branches resolve, your next assistant action must start both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct `./taskctl` processes. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Take statuses only from successful full-record gets.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter task data. Do not expose stable IDs, dates, owners, notes, unrelated matches, or other full-record fields.

`status-note.md` must contain exactly two nonblank, newline-terminated Markdown bullet lines in the requested order, with no heading, blank lines, or other content. Use these exact shapes:
`- Donor Data Cleanup at Advancement Program: <retrieved-status-or-UNRESOLVED>`
`- Exhibit Label Proofing at Museum Program: <retrieved-status-or-UNRESOLVED>; comparison: <MATCH-or-DIFFER-or-UNAVAILABLE>`

Preserve every retrieved status exactly. Use `MATCH` only when both retrieved status strings are identical, `DIFFER` only when both resolve and differ, and `UNAVAILABLE` if either branch is unresolved. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Leave all supplied and protected files unchanged. Return only the same two bullets in your final response.
